entry.title: Weak Quantum Bounty Ceremony
entry.summary:
The concept of using Bitcoin transactions as puzzles has evolved to include mechanisms where the reward can be dynamically increased by other participants. In a typical scenario, an initial amount, such as 10k satoshis, is set as an anchor for the puzzle. This non-trivial sum ensures that the transaction does not fall into the "dust" category and remains viable for enhancement. For instance, creators can design puzzles where they themselves cannot move the coins without solving the puzzle. This setup allows the possibility of increasing the reward post-creation as long as the coins are signed with appropriate sighashes, such as SIGHASH_ANYONECANPAY, which was utilized in a mainnet transaction (see [mempool.space transaction details](https://mempool.space/tx/8349df0753e80cce322322f1b76789e1d0fd6693aed2f4de4e49576423081ae7)).
Further exploration into this field shows that even test coins on networks like Bitcoin's testnet4 and signet are gaining traction and being treated similarly to altcoins. Despite being initially valued at zero, these test chain coins eventually acquire value, suggesting a potential market as seen on platforms like [BitcoinSignet Exchange](https://altquick.com/exchange/market/BitcoinSignet). This development indicates that creating puzzles will always incur some cost, whether it's the computing power required or the nominal currency value associated with the network used.
Moreover, the use of the SIGHASH_ANYONECANPAY signature allows for a flexible transaction structure where additional inputs can be added by anyone. This facilitates community participation where users can increase the transaction's fee or add more inputs by solving simpler, accessible sub-puzzles. Such dynamics not only cement the challenge within the blockchain but also demonstrate the solvability and community engagement around these puzzles. These efforts were illustrated through various transactions across different Bitcoin networks including the mainnet, testnet4, and signet (for detailed transactions, see [mainnet](https://mempool.space/tx/aba3c2ae442aa20150996ee68f9aa4da83b57a4312891078be0c2e68c50b2801), [testnet4](https://mempool.space/testnet4/tx/cc159432ffb7a166abeccc79800e9616a09ea9ac6937080c2ca37b38671970e5), and [signet](https://mempool.space/signet/tx/5aa27f3639c2218ee710fee9830924a129098dca94446661f37cae79c4e76712)). Such innovative uses of blockchain technology highlight its versatility and the growing interest in cryptographic puzzles as both a hobby and a potential investment mechanism.
